VITE_API_URL must be empty at Docker build time
The sidebar nav label for the Configuration page MUST read 'Agent configuration'
The sidebar nav label for the Widget page MUST read 'Widget configuration'
The Team page table header column MUST read 'Team member' instead of 'Member'
The role label for the 'agent' role MUST read 'Escalation agent' instead of 'Agent'
Newly added team members MUST receive a welcome email with a temporary strong password
The conversation filter control background MUST NOT be white in Dark Mode
The chat widget MUST support a Dark Mode option
The Live Preview MUST support a Dark Mode option
The KB page toolbar layout MUST have Search input on top row (left-justified), and Category, Status, Scan for conflicts, Export CSV, Import, and + Add article all on one row below (bottom-justified)
The 'Save changes' control on configuration pages MUST be relabeled 'Save draft inputs'
The tier label 'professional' MUST be capitalized as 'Professional'
Brand voice MUST be a mandatory configuration field
All transient keys, values, URLs, and variables that change between builds or tenant environments MUST NOT be hardcoded; they MUST be retrieved from tenant config database or env.local
Glossary term: 'configuration manager' = the top-level CONFIGURATION control group (Activate/Discard/Roll back)
Glossary term: 'badge' = a small label indicator (e.g., status badge, role badge)
The text 'Agent Red Customer Experience' MUST NOT appear as visible text — only as tooltip/on-hover label or alt text
Quick actions MUST appear in the chat UI on the administration page for preview
There MUST be no visible control for widget drop-shadow on the widget config page (it needs to be added)
Pre-chat form and offline form greeting options MUST have explanations of what they do

Each dashboard display area MUST have a tooltip/link to relevant documentation (Total conversations, Avg response time, Resolution rate, Customer satisfaction, Escalation rate, Daily conversation volume, Recent conversations, Top intents)
Each analytics display area MUST have a tooltip/link to relevant documentation (Intents breakdown, Knowledge gaps, etc.)
The 'Primary color' label MUST be renamed to 'Header left color'
The 'Header gradient end' label MUST be renamed to 'Header right color'
Configurations MUST be nameable and it MUST be possible to select and apply a named saved configuration from a list of previously saved configurations
Prompt template variables MUST be displayed below the Prompt template input box (like greeting message variables), not in a separate tab
When 'Escalation agent' role is selected, the team member MUST also be assigned to one or more escalation categories (Sales, Support, Service, Account, Technical assistance, General inquiry)
It MUST be possible to toggle each team member between enabled and disabled
The Purchase button hover color MUST match the sidebar menu item hover color
In Standard mode (first-time setup), the Go live checklist MUST list mandatory inputs required for system operation
Once Agent Red is active, all Standard mode changes MUST be committed and activated per-page via sidebar navigation
The 'Roll back' option MUST NOT be available if the environment has never been Activated
Test environment testing MUST begin with a Pending state tenant, complete activation, then send test messages before testing Inbox and other pages (Option C)
Knowledge base articles MUST display Category and Status in the article list
Category and Status filters on the KB page MUST return results that match the filter criteria
KB changes MUST participate in the Save-then-Activate flow: KB creates/edits/deletes mark config as Pending, activation deploys KB state alongside other config, Discard reverts KB changes
Quick action creation MUST mark config as Pending in the CONFIGURATION state
The Auto-open control on Quick Actions MUST be functional
The Upgrade path MUST display entitlement tiers in order (Starter -> Pro -> Pro+ -> Enterprise) with an option to Upgrade to each
The Light/Dark toggle MUST retain Dark Mode colors even in Light Mode since the sticky nav header retains Dark Mode colors in Light Mode
All references to the old GitHub repo URL MUST be updated to the new URL: https://github.com/mike-remakerdigital/agent-red

The strategic direction shall prioritize conversation quality over cost reduction, with four priorities in order: (1) quality of individual conversations, (2) consistency across conversations, (3) operational visibility, (4) cost efficiency.
Implement CQ-2: quality threshold alerting that notifies when conversation quality falls below a configurable threshold.
Implement CQ-5: per-topic quality breakdown showing quality scores segmented by conversation topic.

Every new widget_* field must exist in four places: fields.yaml, field_mapping.py, cosmos_schema.py, and the widget TypeScript type definition. Missing from any layer makes the field invisible to the widget.
The setup checklist shall not be shown to tenants that are already active (i.e., have completed initial activation).
The gradient toggle configuration control shall toggle the header gradient effect in the widget.
A Shopify purchase event shall trigger automated tenant provisioning without manual intervention.

The widget shall implement an in-conversation authentication flow with 5 steps: (1) detect auth-required intent, (2) present auth prompt, (3) verify credentials, (4) resume conversation with authenticated context, (5) persist auth state.
The agent pipeline shall use a 6-agent containerized architecture: Intent Classifier, Knowledge Retrieval, Response Generator, Critic/Supervisor, Escalation Handler, Analytics Collector.
Agent Red shall retain its own widget, admin console, and configuration layer (not replaced by AGNTCY equivalents).
The brand primary color shall be #C41E2A (later updated to #ff3621).
Refactoring R2 (response model consolidation) shall be implemented at P1 priority.
Refactoring R5 (Cosmos client singleton) shall be rejected — current pattern is acceptable.
Seven test procedures shall be created: UI, Chrome MCP, load, isolation, security, rate limit, and quality.
Inbox search shall search conversation content (message text), not just metadata fields.
Conversation archival shall be supported as a future feature for moving old conversations to an archived state.
Unknown customers shall be identified by a combination of: session ID, Shopify credentials (if available), email verification, and asserted identities.
The Persistent Customer Memory (PCM) identity system shall depend on customer identification resolution before storing cross-session data.

The configuration activation model shall use a three-state lifecycle: Active (live config) -> Pending (unsaved draft changes) -> Activate (with validation gates). Discard reverts to Active. Roll back restores previous version.
All integration tooltips shall be present and functional on the Integrations page.
Integration name text shall be displayed at 22px font size.
The LUIT-SA (Large UI Test - Standalone Admin) 93-step test procedure shall be executed to completion, fixing all defects encountered until all steps pass.
The Critic agent prompt shall not block responses that contain the customer's own name (false-positive PII blocking).
Conversation history from the current session shall be passed to the response generator to maintain conversational context.
WI #93-96 (Layer 4 Fine-Tuning Pipeline) shall be implemented with a 7-stage pipeline: Collect, Cleanse, Format, Train, Compare, Evaluate, Deploy.
Layer 3 PatternExtractionService shall be integrated into SystemPromptBuilder, injecting pattern context only for Response Generator and Escalation Handler agents.
Session wrap-up shall include updating knowledge base, cleaning artifacts, committing to GitHub, updating the GitHub project and wiki, and providing a next-session initialization prompt.
The 'Deactivate' button text shall not be truncated — the full word must be visible in the sidebar button group.
GDPR compliance webhooks, session token authentication, and App Bridge Save Bar API shall be added to the project backlog.
All required logo image formats and dimensions shall be documented, with a documented procedure for end-to-end logo updates across all project assets.
Cosmos DB partition key shall be tenant_id for all tenant-scoped collections.
NATS topics shall be tenant-scoped using the pattern {tenant_id}.{agent} for conversation pipeline isolation.
Dual authentication shall be supported: Shopify session tokens (signed JWT) for Shopify merchants, API keys (SHA-256 hash) for direct customers.
Per-tenant rate limits shall be tiered by plan: Starter 10/min, Professional 50/min, Enterprise 200/min.
Per-tenant secrets shall be stored in Azure Key Vault using the naming convention tenant-{id}-{type}.
PII scrubbing shall be applied at the logging layer — no customer-identifiable data in Application Insights telemetry.
Consent management shall be implemented for Persistent Customer Memory — consent gates PCM Layers 2-4, denial triggers L2-4 data deletion.
An append-only audit log shall be maintained for all tenant operations.

A work item shall be created to investigate: (A) customer satisfaction impact of reducing P50 response latency below 1,500ms, and (B) cost of options to reduce P50 to minimum including custom AI models and fine-tuning, producing a cost/benefit chart.

The PCM customer profile shall include: (1) historical purchase information (product ID, date, rating/review), (2) historical product questions from previous chats, (3) geographic region codes for geography-aware RAG, (4) marketing segmentation codes for campaign-aware RAG, (5) jurisdiction codes for terms/conditions applicability, (6) historical shopping cart contents (purchased or not).
Publishable widget key authentication (WI-170) shall be implemented with pk_live_ prefix format, SHA-256 hashing, scoped to /api/chat/* and /ws/chat/* paths only.

The Trial tier MUST provide professional-grade entitlements for 14 days: 5,000 conversations, 60 rpm rate limit, 10 max concurrent sessions, 365-day history retention, PCM Layers 1-3, $0.00 overage rate (hard cap), 20 max quick actions, 50 max quick action assignments. After expiry the merchant chooses Starter, Professional, or Enterprise.
The Conversation Inbox API (WI-171) shall provide 5 endpoints on /api/admin/conversations: list, detail, messages, assign agent, and add internal note.
The Knowledge Base CRUD API (WI-175) shall provide 5 endpoints on /api/admin/knowledge: list, detail, create, update, and soft-delete.

The GDPR REST API (WI-180) shall provide 5 endpoints on /api/gdpr: export (tenant/customer scope), delete (with grace period and force override), read consent, update tenant consent, and update customer consent.
Shopify GDPR mandatory webhooks (WI-35) shall be implemented: customers/data_request, customers/redact, shop/redact.
The configuration system MUST use a Save-then-Activate model where changes are saved but not active until explicitly activated
A configuration state check MUST run before activation and fail with an error if the configuration is not sufficiently complete
Multi-tenant scale, security, and failure mode scenarios MUST be tested
Documentation links MUST be included in the Shopify app submission materials
The Inbox page MUST be a read-only transcript viewer — chat input MUST be removed entirely
The 4-role architecture MUST be implemented: superadmin, admin, escalation_agent, viewer
The superadmin role MUST be hidden from the team management UI and cannot be deleted
The viewer role MUST have read-only access to all admin pages
Team members MUST NOT be able to change their own email addresses — email is an admin-controlled identity field
Every tooltip MUST contain brief descriptive text AND a link to the corresponding documentation page with an anchor to the relevant section
Dark mode page background color MUST be #141414
Section heading underlines MUST use the standard box border color (#272727)
Highlighted/selected conversation row background MUST use the sidebar active-page highlight color
The escalation_agent role MUST be restricted to read-only Inbox access only — no access to configuration, team, or settings pages
Per-user API keys MUST follow the format ar_user_* with the key cryptographically linked to a specific team member email
The Activate button MUST make previously saved configuration changes live and active for the widget
Widget Config page MUST also participate in the Save-then-Activate state model alongside Agent Config, KB, and Quick Actions
Error messages shown to users MUST be user-friendly — not technical internal strings like '_system: No draft to activate'.

When a tenant admin attempts to activate configuration without a saved draft, the error message MUST read: 'Save your configuration first. Go to Agent Configuration, review your settings, and click Save draft inputs before activating.'
Tier-gated features (e.g., Memory & Privacy pro+ fields) MUST NOT be submitted to the API when the tenant is on a lower tier. The frontend MUST filter out tier-gated fields before save.
A Protected Behaviors registry MUST be maintained to prevent uncontrolled regressions. Behaviors with machine-verifiable assertions MUST be checked before every build.
Administrators MUST have 2FA enabled by default, with explicit opt-out only if the superadmin allows it.
Escalation agents (non-administrators) do NOT require 2FA because they cannot alter system configuration.
Administrators MUST have all of the same access as the superadmin, with the exception that the superadmin account is not visible to any other user and cannot be disabled.
All non-administrators MUST only have permission to interact with the inbox.
Team member deletion MUST be a hard delete — no soft delete.
Configuration MUST NOT be 'Active' when mandatory inputs are missing. The system MUST prevent activation without all required fields.
The seed script MUST initialize tenants with config_state='draft', NOT 'active'.
Session wrap-up MUST include: knowledge base updates, cleanup of unnecessary artifacts, commit and push to GitHub, GitHub project/wiki updates, and a next-session initialization prompt.
The chat widget MUST NOT display on the admin console when AI Configuration is not Active. The widget visibility MUST be tied to the activation status.
All team member actions (addition, deletion, role change, status change) MUST be stored in an audit log
While a tenancy is Expired, the widget key MUST be disabled (not allowed)
If a merchant administrator interacts with the activation control while in 'Expired' state, a pop-up MUST offer the option to purchase a Starter, Professional or Enterprise license

There MUST be a limit (time, total conversation memory/storage) on the number of conversations in the inbox, beyond which conversations are automatically archived (not searchable, but exportable as CSV)

Session wrap-up MUST include: detailed knowledge base updates, repeatable procedure confirmation, artifact cleanup, agentredcx.com updates, git commit/push, GitHub project/wiki updates, production deployment (unless advised against), and next-session prompt
Elements that cannot be fully functionally evaluated offline MUST be noted in the knowledge base for production verification
Plan-gated elements MUST be tested per entitlement tier — a full set of tests may be needed for each plan (Trial, Starter, Professional, Enterprise)
A master list of all required tooltips and their documentation URLs MUST be maintained and addressed as a single work item
The role permissions tooltip content MUST be arranged in a table with vertically consistent content rows
The 'Inactive' state change MUST be triggered when the admin saves configuration changes that remove mandatory fields — must work as a client-server round trip, not just client-side
The Knowledge Base 'Import' control MUST have a tooltip
SPA stands for 'Service Provider Administrator' (Remaker Digital employee), NOT 'Single Page Application'. Superadmin/admin refers to merchant users (customers), NOT Remaker Digital employees.
The test tenant MUST be created with superadmin email 'info@remakerdigital.com' to allow API key reset requests, starting in initialized state

Escalation emails MUST be sent to team members with distinct email addresses that are manually verifiable via clickable links that add authenticated email addresses to conversation identity metadata shown in the Inbox detail area
Once the Simulated Customer Tenant is created, its known state MUST make it easier to test non-disruptive upgrade, entitlement changes, and license entitlement enforcement
The Role permissions block on the Team page MUST be relocated to a tooltip on the 'Role' column header — it MUST NOT be displayed as a page section
The '+ Invite member' button MUST be grouped with the Team member list
The Dashboard test mode filter (All/Production/Test switcher) MUST be removed since test mode no longer exists
Integration logo containers MUST use 180px x 180px dimensions with border-radius 8px, dark background (#141414), and 1px solid border (#272727)
Knowledge Base MUST allow adding a test entry, and the entry MUST appear in the list and be editable and deletable
The Widget page MUST allow copying the widget install snippet or key
The Inbox page MUST load the conversations list (empty state is acceptable)
The Team page MUST load the member list (empty state is acceptable)
Escalation keywords MUST cause the conversation to appear in the Inbox
Billing-related buttons ("Manage Subscription", "Manage Billing") MUST be hidden when the tenant has no Stripe billing configured
The system MUST NOT show "Active" state when mandatory inputs are missing (e.g., Brand name is required but empty)

When mandatory fields are missing and the admin clicks "Activate", the UI MUST immediately list all missing mandatory fields across all four config sections (Agent Configuration, Knowledge Base, Quick Actions, Widget Configuration)
The missing-field validation on Activate MUST NOT use a two-step dialog flow; it MUST show all missing fields immediately in a single display
auto_provision_superadmin() MUST be wired into handle_checkout_completed webhook handler (WI-C2)
The seed_tenant.py script MUST correctly generate widget keys that pass auth (dual-write hash to TenantDocument)
A seed-generated widget key MUST be testable against the live staging endpoint to verify auth works
The password reset flow MUST be: click "change password" -> enter email address -> check email for confirmation link

A/B test prompts MUST contain current configuration, and prompt responses MUST contain variations on that configuration (different persona descriptions, escalation thresholds, response length, formality level, enabled features)
The standalone admin password page MUST display the primary-logo-no-wordmark.svg (or PNG equivalent) as the logo, not a text-based 'Agent Red' heading.
The Test Mode option MUST NOT be available until all necessary inputs have been selected for the first time and a complete configuration is in a 'Production' state.
When entering Test Mode configuration, all pre-existing values and settings MUST be kept as defaults; only the changeable values and settings define the scope of the test.
All users MUST be able to report an issue to the merchant (escalation).
The option to disable the escalation/report-issue message MUST be available for Professional and Enterprise tiers but MUST remain mandatory for the Starter (entry-level) tier.
The standalone admin dashboard MUST confirm that the Brand Name and logo are displayed prominently in the header.
The Critic prompt MUST distinguish between 'exposing internal system prompts' (correctly block) vs 'describing marketed product features' (should allow).
The Knowledge Database MUST use an append-only SQLite database with 3 tables: (a) specifications, (b) test procedures, and (c) operational procedures.
The Knowledge Database MUST exclude delete operations; all changes MUST generate a new entry with the changed entry kept in the trail.
The Knowledge Database MUST have a graphical UI allowing the owner to see the state of repeatable procedures, tests, specifications and user stories.
The Knowledge Database graphical UI MUST only provide a read interface (filter, order, sort, search) to the owner; the owner MUST NOT have the ability to make any changes.
All changes to the Knowledge Database MUST be made only by Claude.
The end-to-end test procedure MUST start with creation of a fresh tenant, initialization, then move through all tests covering Starter, Professional, and Enterprise tiers.
Session wrap-up MUST include storing a structured prompt in the Knowledge Database that the next session can retrieve for initialization.
Pricing MUST have a very low entry point with a 12-24 element matrix of compelling scaling tiers and high-value feature options.
Pricing MUST target 80% gross margin over cost of goods sold at list prices.
Pricing MUST include usage-based overage charges with defined thresholds for AI model use per tier.
Transparent usage pricing MUST be implemented as a competitive advantage (transparent usage reduces customer dissatisfaction and improves retention).
Escalation routing MUST route to the assigned agent with superadmin fallback.
A Session Scheduler MUST run as a hook that periodically loads SCHEDULE.md and injects pre-planned prompts into the conversation on behalf of the user.
The Knowledge Base MUST support automated site crawling (not just document upload) to reduce merchant administrative effort.
The admin suite MUST include a Knowledge Base conflict/duplication scanner tool that identifies conflicts or duplications and provides instructions for resolution.
The email template MUST include text below the WIDGET KEY: 'Your chat widget key and API URL are available from your admin console.'
The email template MUST NOT have inner boxes with corners with a large radius; objects in the email template MUST NOT have rounded corners.

The grouped configuration items box MUST include a control displaying the state of those grouped items and allowing 'Activate' changes, 'Roll-back' changes, or 'Discard' changes to restore all inputs to their currently Active state.
A tooltip MUST be added to the 'Professional' plan-type indicator in the sticky top navbar explaining what the plan means and what subscription plans are available.
The four-layer personalization stack (Persistent Customer Memory) MUST be highlighted in marketing materials, documentation, website and anywhere business value pillars are enumerated.
A fresh deployment MUST be in its initialized state with zero conversations and not in 'Active' state.
Web crawlers MUST be blocked from triggering billable conversations OR crawler conversations MUST be classified as non-billable.

When escalating a conversation, the admin MUST either (a) choose a category causing Agent Red to send an escalation email to one of the Escalation agents handling that category, or (b) choose a specific Escalation agent as the target.
Once an escalation category or specific Escalation agent has been selected, that user MUST receive an escalation email with information about the escalation.
Once a conversation has been escalated, the assigned Team member MUST be the only Team member with permission to operate the 'Resolve conversation' control for that conversation.
The 'Escalation agent' role MUST ensure correct routing of escalations to Team members based on escalation category, for both manual and AI-initiated escalations.
The conversation status 'Completed' MUST be renamed to 'Resolved' for consistency with the 'Resolve conversation' control label.
When a tenant is initialized, there MUST NOT be any articles; initialization MUST verify that all existing articles are removed.
The Quick Actions 'Status' column MUST be wide enough to fully display status badges without truncation (e.g., 'Active' MUST NOT show as 'Act...').

Testing Repeatable Procedures MUST include tests that verify: (1) test PII injected into a conversation is correctly scrubbed, (2) consent prompt appears, (3) a GDPR deletion request successfully deletes all system information for the identified customer.
Technical work (implementation, testing, new capabilities) MUST have elevated priority over creative/content work (marketing, cosmetic).

The system MUST implement a four-layer personalization stack: (1) Session context, (2) Cross-session customer memory, (3) Vectorized interaction history for customer-specific RAG, (4) Periodic retraining on accumulated cleansed data.
The Shopify storefront MUST be configured with up-to-date product descriptions, polished pages, and template matching Agent Red brand colors and visual style before designer engagement.
Conversation filter counts MUST be consistent: the sum of all status filter counts MUST equal the 'All' conversations count.
A fail-closed Critic policy MUST be implemented as designed.
The superadmin MUST only be visible to the superadmin — other users MUST NOT see the superadmin team member in the UI
The role name 'Agent' MUST be changed to 'Escalation agent'
SPA tenant provisioning inputs MUST include: Merchant Name, merchant URL, merchant superadmin email, and entitlement type (Trial, Starter, Professional, or Enterprise)

The customer identity flow MUST follow this sequence: (1) AI greeting and Quick Actions presented when chat opens, (2) customer inputs message or selects Quick Action, (3) AI checks Shopify auth and whether email is known — if NOT, AI requests it, (4) customer chooses anonymous or provides email, (5) if email provided, OTP sent, customer enters code, authenticated
Customer identity flow is a P0 requirement along with SPA console UI for new tenant provisioning
Seed data MUST NOT include team members — the only team member in a freshly provisioned tenancy MUST be the superadmin logging in for the first time
Beta tenant provisioning MUST use SPA manual provisioning via the Provider Console, not the seed script
The beta is of no value if Persistent Customer Memory features are not working correctly — PCM MUST NOT be deferred
The vectorize_conversation background scanner MUST use Option A (background scanner) to wire vectorization
The Knowledge Database MUST never delete any records — retention policy is 'never delete' with ~400GB budget (~57,000 years of history)
Periodic audit sessions MUST occur with a clean context for self-care and inventory/inspection of assets and plans
Every 5th session (S100, S105, ...) MUST be automatically flagged as an audit session using handoff-embedded cadence (Option A)
The pipeline budget MUST reflect a P50 of 7,000ms and a timeout at 8,000ms
The SSE identity stream MUST send a synthetic 'stage' event before 'token' events so the widget creates a message bubble for identity preprocessor responses
Error messages in the activation dialog MUST handle both string errors and {field_name, message} object errors — no [object Object] display
PCM feature MUST be fully operational for the beta — vectorization scanner, consent handling, and memory retrieval all working
The RRF (Reciprocal Rank Fusion) scores MUST be normalized to a 0-1 range so they can exceed the MIN_RELEVANCE_SCORE threshold
The KB seed script MUST be idempotent — running it multiple times MUST NOT create duplicate entries
The Add article dialog MUST allow upload of documents (not just text entry)
Knowledge Database session_prompts table MUST follow append-only event sourcing pattern — no UPDATE or DELETE operations
The Stripe product catalog MUST include subscription tiers (Starter, Professional, Enterprise) with monthly and annual pricing plus metered overage using Stripe Billing Meters
The Stripe product catalog MUST include conversation packs (1K, 5K, 20K) and add-on modules (Multi-Language, Advanced Analytics, Priority Support)
Shopify and Stripe partner program approval criteria and enhanced relationship benefits MUST be documented
The AI MUST proactively ask for the customer's email address in its FIRST response per the P0 identity mandate
The widget_greeting_message field MUST NOT be set to empty string during deactivation — empty string overrides platform defaults, while None allows defaults to apply
Vector search and BM25 MUST be parallelized using asyncio.gather() for knowledge retrieval performance
Analytics page title is 'Analytics'
Analytics SegmentedControl offers options: 7d, 7d, 14d, 14d, 30d, 30d
Billing has 'Purchase' button
Billing has 'Manage subscription' button
Billing has 'Manage billing' button
Billing page title is 'Billing & usage'
Billing page title is 'Billing & usage'
Configuration has 'Configuration name' input field
Configuration has 'Brand name' input field
Configuration has 'Brand voice' input field
Configuration has 'Formality' input field
Configuration has 'Response length' input field

Hook 'assertion-check.py' for session automation

The intent classification system MUST recognize 17 customer intent categories: general_inquiry, product_question, order_status, return_request, exchange_request, refund_request, shipping_inquiry, pricing_question, availability_check, complaint, feedback, account_issue, payment_issue, subscription_question, technical_support, greeting, escalation. Unrecognized intents MUST default to general_inquiry.

The Starter tier MUST include: 1,000 conversations/month, 60 rpm rate limit, 5 max concurrent sessions, 90-day history retention, PCM Layers 1-2 only, $0.04/conversation overage rate, 5 max quick actions, 10 max quick action assignments.

The Professional tier MUST include: 5,000 conversations/month, 60 rpm rate limit, 10 max concurrent sessions, 365-day history retention, PCM Layers 1-3, $0.025/conversation overage rate, 20 max quick actions, 50 max quick action assignments.

The Enterprise tier MUST include: 20,000 conversations/month, 240 rpm rate limit, 30 max concurrent sessions, unlimited history retention, PCM Layers 1-4 (including dedicated model training), $0.015/conversation overage rate, 50 max quick actions, 200 max quick action assignments.
